title: TypeError: Excel.Workbook is not a constructor
author: tvrashid

comments.author: tvrashid
comments.body: I get this most of the time when using excel js inside node application

comments.author: Tenison
comments.body: How do you solve this problem

comments.author: sagungargs15
alexfauquette
comments.body: @sagungargs15 If you're using vite or remix, the [PR linked to this issue](https://github.com/mui/mui-x/issues/5035) could be helpfull.

comments.author: Tenison
sagungargs15
comments.body:
@Tenison I am also using exceljs to import a file in .xlsx format in ReactJs + Typescript. I am also attaching the file for reference here. The challenge is my code is failing at the `const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook(); `stage because of the following error 

```
[invoice.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/10122718/invoice.xlsx)

index.tsx:86 Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: exceljs__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.Workbook is not a constructor
    at FileReader.<anonymous> (index.tsx:86:1)
```

comments.author: vibhash1083
comments.body: Any solution for this issue? I am facing same issue with reactjs + typescript.

comments.author: tonioriol
comments.body:
This was the solution for me, seems to happen in quite a bit of other imports too in a nuxt/nitro project:

```js
import * as ExcelJS from 'exceljs'

const workbook = new ExcelJS.default.Workbook()
```
As mentioned in this other issue https://github.com/mui/mui-x/issues/5035#issuecomment-1156240336, "the excelJs is imported, but set into a default property. So the constructor is not excelJS.Workbook but excelJS.default.Workbook"

comments.author: vibhash1083
comments.body:
> ```js
> import * as ExcelJS from 'exceljs'
> 
> const workbook = new ExcelJS.default.Workbook()
> ```

I am getting following error while using this:

Property 'default' does not exist on type 'typeof import("node_modules/exceljs/index")'.ts(2339)

comments.author: tonioriol
comments.body:
@vibhash1083 sorry to hear that. I would suggest what is mentioned in the issue comment I linked in my previous response. Do the import as I show and try to console log and see the actual shape of the ExcelJS variable.

But reading again your error output, it says “does not exist in **type**” so are you doing the import exactly the way is shown in my previous comment? Cause it seems you are doing `import type...`.

comments.body:

comments.author: kztera
comments.body:
> Any solution for this issue? I am facing same issue with reactjs + typescript.

I unchecked Disable cache in Networking tab and problem solved

![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/116504460/08064246-cb65-43b9-a162-fcff75a78cae)

comments.author: WilberthGamboa
musthafa1996
AdamGaskins
comments.body:
Instead of 

```js
import * as ExcelJS from 'exceljs'
```

try

```js
import ExcelJS from 'exceljs'
```
